_doc.purpose: Canonical-name -> synonyms map. This is the redirect table: the many names an algorithm travels under all resolve to one canonical entry. The atlas search resolves aliases, and each future per-algorithm page will 301 its alias slugs here with a top-of-page note explaining the canonical name.
_doc.schema: Every key except those starting with underscore is a canonical name that MUST match some atlas entry's `a` (unless redirectOnly:true). Value: { aka: [alternate names], note?: string, redirectOnly?: true }. An `aka` that is itself a distinct real entry is a MERGE CANDIDATE; the check prints those as a planning warning rather than a synonym.
_doc.resolveNote: Keep this file lean and correct. A wrong synonym is worse than a missing one: it collapses two genuinely different methods.

Union-Find.note: Universally abbreviated DSU in competitive programming.

K-means.note: Lloyd's algorithm is the standard iterative solver for k-means.

Monte Carlo tree search.note: MCTS with the UCB1 selection rule is specifically called UCT (Upper Confidence bounds applied to Trees).

Dijkstra's algorithm.note: Uniform-cost search is Dijkstra's algorithm in AI-search terminology.

Latent Dirichlet allocation.note: Distinct from Linear Discriminant Analysis, also abbreviated LDA.

Stochastic gradient descent.note: Appears as a heuristic (the optimizer) on Backpropagation entries rather than a standalone entry.

Viterbi algorithm.note: Merge candidate demonstrator: the same dynamic program appears domain-specialized as Viterbi DP, Viterbi decoding, and Viterbi tagging across DP, coding, and NLP families. Kept separate as distinct lessons for now; this note marks the relationship.

Lempel-Ziv complexity.note: The former compression-coding entry 'Lempel-Ziv-complexity estimation' was merged into this canonical entry (2026-07-21); the retired name stays as a redirect.

Expectation propagation.note: The former machine-learning standalone entry was merged into the statistics-inference pair with moment matching (2026-07-21).

Christofides.note: The scheduling-operations copy (same method, same 'Metric TSP approximation' problem, reworded heuristic) was merged here 2026-07-21; approximation is the home topic.

Smith-Waterman.note: The dynamic-programming copy was identical (h null, same problem) and was merged here 2026-07-21; the DP structure is taught on the page, not as a second catalog unit.

Seam carving.note: Merged from dynamic-programming 2026-07-21; signal-image is the home topic.

Li Chao tree.note: Merged from dynamic-programming 2026-07-21; search-structures is the home topic.

External merge sort.note: The databases-query entry was renamed to this canonical spelling 2026-07-21; it stays a distinct unit (a different problem phrase, 'Large-relation sorting') under one consistent algorithm name.

Longest common subsequence.note: Two live units share this canonical name and are genuinely distinct lessons: the plain DP for sequence similarity, and Hunt-Szymanski for diff computation.
